subject: Watch President Trump speak at Suresnes Cemetery in France
body:
On November 11, 1918, the United States joined its fellow Allies in signing an Armistice with Germany that officially ended...

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d662c14a96&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 13, 2018

** The 100th anniversary of World War I’s end
------------------------------------------------------------
On November 11, 1918, the United States joined its fellow Allies in signing an Armistice with Germany that officially ended the fighting in World War I. Exactly 100 years to the day later, President Donald J. Trump stood at Suresnes American Cemetery (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c5662831c&e=3ee4985eea) near Paris, France, honoring the 116,000 Americans who died in that war.

“Here on the revered grounds of Suresnes American Cemetery lie more than 1,500 U.S. service members who made the ultimate sacrifice in the First World War,” President Trump said. “In that treacherous forest and the surrounding fields, American Marines, soldiers, and Allied Forces fought—and they fought through hell—to turn the tide of the war.”

In Paris, the President and First Lady joined French President Emmanuel Macron, his wife First Lady Brigitte Macron, and other world leaders for the Centennial Commemoration of Armistice Day at the Arc de Triomphe.

“The American and French patriots of World War I embody the timeless virtues of our two republics: honor and courage; strength and valor; love and loyalty; grace and glory,” President Trump said. “It is our duty to preserve the civilization they defended and to protect the peace they so nobly gave their lives to secure one century ago.”

Watch President Trump’s full remarks at Suresnes American Cemetery. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=32b499de88&e=3ee4985eea)

More: Watch First Lady Melania Trump join Brigitte Macron for Armistice Day (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a68e0c8753&e=3ee4985eea)

** 3 big ways President Trump is putting veterans first
------------------------------------------------------------
“To the 20 million veterans alive today: this Veterans Day, we recommit ourselves to providing you with the care you deserve,” President Trump writes in his 2018 Veterans Day Proclamation (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=23ec5aed34&e=3ee4985eea) .

This President’s commitment to veterans goes beyond rhetoric. In addition to rebuilding America’s military strength to better serve the heroes of today, the Trump Administration has made caring for U.S. veterans a top priority in three big ways:
1. Honoring our veterans’ service: "We stand up for America. We stand up for the patriots who defend America. And we stand up for our National Anthem (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cfc5ad2ffc&e=3ee4985eea) ,” President Trump told Veterans of Foreign Wars this summer.

2. Reforming veterans care: The President signed the VA Mission Act into law in June, which enacts sweeping reforms to modernize veterans care and give patients access to walk-in treatment.

3. Holding the VA accountable: From the start of the Trump Administration through the end of this May, the Department of Veterans Affairs took more than 3,200 accountability actions against employees for misconduct or poor performance, resulting in more than 2,500 removals from positions.

The facts: How President Trump is honoring his commitment to our veterans (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=35182ba1c4&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian
President Donald J. Trump participates in a Diwali ceremonial lighting of the Diya in the Roosevelt Room of the White House | November 13, 2018

subject: Record business optimism, the Republican Senate, and Vice President Pence in Tokyo
body:
The record level of economic optimism among small businesses is continuing, and those firms trying to fill jobs is at a 45-year high... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fbc4ff9170&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Boom: Record High Business Optimism, Need for Employees at 45-Year High**

“The record level of economic optimism among small businesses is continuing, and those firms trying to fill jobs is at a 45-year high,” Paul Bedard reports.

“The National Federation of Independent Businesses said the optimism has only been this high three times in the last 43 years and has consistently been high all year long . . . In addition, companies are planning to expand and add inventory.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=41fe101f35&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=642a6d2d34&e=3ee4985eea), Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) lays out how the expanded Republican Senate majority plans to help govern in the coming two years—if House Democrats don’t opt for fanning the flames of division instead. “We’ll keep working to lift the burden on American job creators and small businesses. We’ll stay focused on helping communities across the country seize new opportunities and realize greater prosperity. We’ll seek new ways to make life easier for working families.”
---
|
---
| While visiting Tokyo, Vice President Mike Pence joined Prime Minister Shinzo Abe yesterday and “reaffirmed the need to keep sanctions on North Korea to achieve its denuclearization as they showcased their bilateral alliance, while Pence also urged Japan to do more to reduce the U.S. trade deficit,” Mari Yamaguchi reports for _[The Associated Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8d3dd24cd8&e=3ee4985eea)_. The Vice President added that “the U.S.-Japan alliance is a ‘cornerstone’ of the region’s peace and prosperity.”
---

[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa602/34a064cc/54eeced/70c11b9/3006251910/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friend: Voter suppression is not just a relic of the past. It is happening today, in Georgia and across the country. That's why it is so important that people share their personal experiences with voter suppression - to remind all of us that the fight is not over, the battle is not won. We have one story to share with you today. Meet JaKayla: [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa602/34a064cc/54eeced/70c11c6/3006251910/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE0MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11142018-full") JaKayla is a freshman at Albany State and a first-time voter. She wanted to vote early, but she didn't have a ride to the polls, so she decided to wait until Election Day when there would be a polling location on her college campus. But when JaKayla went to vote on Election Day, she was told she had to vote by provisional ballot. She asked if she needed to go to a different polling location and was told "No," her only option was to vote using a provisional ballot. Her grandmother even called the Secretary of State's office to ask what they could do to help, but all they would say is "I can't answer that," or "I don't know." So JaKayla called our Voter Protection Hotline to report what happened. Many of JaKayla's classmates at Albany State were forced to cast provisional ballots, and many more, including JaKayla's roommate, were told they could not vote at all. **Our team is not just fighting for Stacey Abrams. We are fighting for Georgians like JaKayla who deserve to have their votes counted.** Because if we don't stand up to agents of voter suppression today, they will continue to attack our civil rights tomorrow, and the next day, and the day after that. That's why we are fighting back, and why we have to ask: **[Will you chip in $3 to support our team as we do battle to defend democracy in Georgia? This fight will be long and hard, but your vote, and JaKayla's vote, is worth it.](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa602/34a064cc/54eeced/70c11c6/3006251910/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTE0MjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-november?refcode=IE11142018-full")** We'll leave you with this: What do we want the history books to say about us? That we backed down in the face of a challenge, or that we fought with our hearts and souls bared to defend democracy? Sincerely, \- Team Abrams
---

name: Ben Jealous
subject: The fight's not over
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
Stacey and Andrew need our help.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790fb/7ab0e2c4/7e739439/3499aac9/3558454956/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-abrams-gillum?refcode=em_OHQ11148A") Friend, My election might be over -- but Stacey and Andrew's are still going strong. From the start, Stacey Abrams and Andrew Gillum have been committed to lifting up the voices of all Georgians and Floridians who have been unseen and unheard by political leaders. But now, after an election that made national news due to its irregularities and voter suppression tactics, Republicans are trying to crown themselves before every vote has been counted. Let's also be honest about something: In both Florida and Georgia, the GOP strategy included targeted, blatant racism, and the most glaring part of that strategy was voter suppression. Voter ID laws, long lines, and broken voting machines disproportionately target communities of color -- also, those communities are more likely to vote for Democrats. **We can still fight back by ensuring that every single vote is counted in Florida and Georgia, because when they are, I think both Andrew and Stacey will win. But we need to help fund the fight for those recounts:** [**Donate $5 or more now to support Stacey Abrams in Georgia and Andrew Gillum in Florida and to make sure every vote is counted.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790fb/7ab0e2c4/7e739439/3499aac9/3558454956/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-abrams-gillum?refcode=em_OHQ11148A") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately and will be split evenly:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790fb/7ab0e2c4/7e739439/3499aac8/3558454956/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-abrams-gillum?refcode=em_OHQ11148A&amount=5&express_lane=true")

---
We can't forget how we got here:
In Georgia, Republican gubernatorial candidate Brian Kemp used his position as Secretary of State to purge more than 100,000 people from the voter registration rolls. He put another 53,000 voter registration applications "on hold" -- 70% of which were from black Georgians. And when people showed up to vote in predominantly black counties, they faced impossibly long lines thanks the closure of 214 polling places since 2012, as well as faulty voting machines. After the election, we learned that 700 voting machines were left wrapped and unused in a nearby warehouse in Atlanta. My friend Stacey spoke up about all of this, before and after the election, only for her opponent to ignore it.
And in Florida, in addition to purges and polling place closures, there were widespread reports suggesting thousands of voters never received the absentee ballots they requested, and absentee ballots that were submitted by black and Latinx voters were rejected at higher rates due to "signature mismatch." Andrew called out his Republican opponent, Ron DeSantis, for taking donations from and speaking at conferences hosted by white supremacists, "I'm not calling Mr. DeSantis a racist. I'm simply saying the racists believe he's a racist." Tuesday's mess proved it once more, too.
There is power in voting, and Republicans know it. Which is why they've done everything they can here to block folks from having their voices heard or their votes counted -- but if we step up and come together, we might just be able to stop them.
[**Let's fight to make sure every single vote is counted in Florida and Georgia -- donate $5 or more now to support the recount efforts of Andrew Gillum and Stacey Abrams.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790fb/7ab0e2c4/7e739439/3499aac9/3558454956/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-abrams-gillum?refcode=em_OHQ11148A")
Thanks,
Ben

| **Senator Young Elected to Lead National Republican Senatorial Committee! **
Senator Todd Young is adding his name to the growing list of Hoosiers doing big things in government and politics! **That's because he was just elected as the chairman of the National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC)!**

As the NRSC's chairman, **Senator Young will lead efforts to maintain and expand Republican control of the U.S. Senate in 2020**.

Todd Young knows a thing or two about beating Democrats -- defeating Evan Bayh in 2016, and this year helping Indiana defeat Joe Donnelly and elect Mike Braun as our next U.S. Senator! In fact, with his skills on the campaign trail,**Senator Young has now risen to become Indiana's senior senator in just two years! **

Watching his resounding victory in 2016, **Senator Young knows what it takes to win a tough race** , and he'll take those skills with him as he helps Republicans win more big Senate races in 2020.

From Vice President Mike Pence at the White House, Dan Coats leading national intelligence, Alex Azar heading up the Department of Health and Human Services and countless others making a big impact, **Hoosiers are doing big things in Washington** , and we can't wait to see the great victories that Senator Young will help Republicans achieve through his new, expanded role!
**Congratulations, Senator Young!!**

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: thoughts?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dan Lipinski for Congress are not tax deductible
body:
| Hey Peter,

What a great election cycle this has been!

Democrats took back the House, a handful of competitive races across the country are still counting ballots, and voters from California to Kansas ushered in a new slate of candidates with strong convictions on healthcare, education, and how to create jobs.

100,000 Signatures Needed to Protect Robert Mueller._** [**ADD YOUR SIGNATURE >>**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cfb/622b7b5b/1e195fe6/307b6019/3719330600/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e181112m") I don't think it's any coincidence that President Trump essentially fired Attorney General Jeff Sessions less than a day after losing the House majority -- the majority that was protecting him from accountability. **And I don't think it's a coincidence that his replacement, Matthew Whitaker, thinks the Mueller investigation goes too far and has talked about ways to undermine it.** | | | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cfb/622b7b5b/1e195fe6/307b6019/3719330600/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e181112m")

It's time for Congress to take action to protect Robert Mueller's investigation. The American people deserve to know the truth about the Trump campaign's ties to Russia, and Mueller must be allowed to finish his work uninterrupted.
[**Help us turn up the grassroots heat on Congress. Demand protection for Robert Mueller and the Russia investigation.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cfb/622b7b5b/1e195fe6/307b6019/3719330600/VEsF/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e181112m")
Thank you for fighting with me.
Debbie
